title: oxalis umbraticola
intro: Oxalis umbraticola, also known as the purple wood sorrel or umbrella plant, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high concentration of anthraquinones. The health benefits of Oxalis umbraticola are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Oxalis umbraticola can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
